Functions.Name: Hypotenuse
Functions.Description: Returns the hypotenuse of a right-angled triangle given the width and height.

Functions.Name: GridSnap_Float
Functions.Description:
Snaps a value to the nearest grid multiple. E.g.,
	 		Location = 5.1, GridSize = 10.0 : return value = 10.0
	  If GridSize is 0 Location is returned
	  if GridSize is very small precision issues may occur.

Functions.Name: Abs
Functions.Description: Returns the absolute (positive) value of A

Functions.Name: Sin
Functions.Description: Returns the sine of A (expects Radians)

Functions.Name: Asin
Functions.Description: Returns the inverse sine (arcsin) of A (result is in Radians)

Functions.Name: Cos
Functions.Description: Returns the cosine of A (expects Radians)

Functions.Name: Acos
Functions.Description: Returns the inverse cosine (arccos) of A (result is in Radians)

Functions.Name: Tan

Functions.Name: Atan
Functions.Description: Returns the inverse tan (atan) (result is in Radians)

Functions.Name: Atan2
Functions.Description: Returns the inverse tan (atan2) of AB (result is in Radians)

Functions.Name: Exp
Functions.Description: Returns exponential(e) to the power A (e^A)

Functions.Name: Log
Functions.Description: Returns log of A base B (if B^R == A, returns R)

Functions.Name: Loge
Functions.Description: Returns natural log of A (if e^R == A, returns R)

Functions.Name: Sqrt
Functions.Description: Returns square root of A

Functions.Name: Square
Functions.Description: Returns square of A (AA)

Functions.Description: Generate a random number between Min and Max

Functions.Name: GetPI

Functions.Description: Returns the value of TAU (= 2  PI)

Functions.Name: DegreesToRadians
Functions.Description: Returns radians value based on the input degrees

Functions.Name: RadiansToDegrees
Functions.Description: Returns degrees value based on the input radians

Functions.Name: DegSin
Functions.Description: Returns the sin of A (expects Degrees)

Functions.Name: DegAsin
Functions.Description: Returns the inverse sin (arcsin) of A (result is in Degrees)

Functions.Name: DegCos
Functions.Description: Returns the cos of A (expects Degrees)

Functions.Name: DegAcos
Functions.Description: Returns the inverse cos (arccos) of A (result is in Degrees)

Functions.Name: DegTan
Functions.Description: Returns the tan of A (expects Degrees)

Functions.Name: DegAtan
Functions.Description: Returns the inverse tan (atan) (result is in Degrees)

Functions.Name: DegAtan2
Functions.Description: Returns the inverse tan (atan2) of AB (result is in Degrees)

Functions.Name: ClampAngle
Functions.Description: Clamps an arbitrary angle to be between the given angles.  Will clamp to nearest boundary.

Params.Name: MinAngleDegrees
Params.Description: "from" angle that defines the beginning of the range of valid angles (sweeping clockwise)

Params.Name: MaxAngleDegrees
Params.Description: "to" angle that defines the end of the range of valid angles

Return.Description: Returns clamped angle in the range -180..180.

Functions.Name: FMin
Functions.Description: Returns the minimum value of A and B

Functions.Name: FMax
Functions.Description: Returns the maximum value of A and B

Functions.Name: FClamp
Functions.Description: Returns Value clamped between A and B (inclusive)

Functions.Name: MaxOfIntArray
Functions.Description: Returns max of all array entries and the index at which it was found. Returns value of 0 and index of -1 if the supplied array is empty.

Functions.Name: MinOfIntArray
Functions.Description: Returns min of all array entries and the index at which it was found. Returns value of 0 and index of -1 if the supplied array is empty.

Functions.Name: MaxOfFloatArray
Functions.Description: Returns max of all array entries and the index at which it was found. Returns value of 0 and index of -1 if the supplied array is empty.

Functions.Name: MinOfFloatArray
Functions.Description: Returns min of all array entries and the index at which it was found. Returns value of 0 and index of -1 if the supplied array is empty.

Functions.Name: MaxOfByteArray
Functions.Description: Returns max of all array entries and the index at which it was found. Returns value of 0 and index of -1 if the supplied array is empty.

Functions.Name: MinOfByteArray
Functions.Description: Returns min of all array entries and the index at which it was found. Returns value of 0 and index of -1 if the supplied array is empty.

Functions.Name: Lerp
Functions.Description: Linearly interpolates between A and B based on Alpha (100% of A when Alpha=0 and 100% of B when Alpha=1)

Functions.Name: InverseLerp
Functions.Description:
Returns the fraction (alpha) of the range B-A that corresponds to Value, e.g.,
		inputs A = 0, B = 8, Value = 3 : outputs Return Value = 38, indicating Value is 38 from A to B 
		inputs A = 8, B = 0, Value = 3 : outputs Return Value = 58, indicating Value is 58 from A to B
	 Named InverseLerp because Lerp( A, B, InverseLerp(A, B, Value) ) == Value

Params.Name: A
Params.Description: The "from" value this float could be, usually but not necessarily a minimum. Returned as 0.

Params.Name: B
Params.Description: The "to" value this float could be, usually but not necessarily a maximum. Returned as 1.

Params.Name: Value
Params.Description: A value intended to be normalized relative to B-A

Return.Description: A normalized alpha value considering A and B.

Functions.Name: Ease
Functions.Description: Easeing  between A and B using a specified easing function

Functions.Name: Round
Functions.Description: Rounds A to the nearest integer

Functions.Name: FFloor
Functions.Description: Rounds A to the largest previous integer

Functions.Name: FTrunc
Functions.Description: Rounds A to an integer with truncation towards zero.  (e.g. -1.7 truncated to -1, 2.8 truncated to 2)

Functions.Name: FTruncVector
Functions.Description: Rounds A to an integer with truncation towards zero for each element in a vector.  (e.g. -1.7 truncated to -1, 2.8 truncated to 2)

Functions.Name: FCeil
Functions.Description: Rounds A to the smallest following integer

Functions.Name: FMod
Functions.Description: Returns the number of times Divisor will go into Dividend (i.e., Dividend divided by Divisor), as well as the remainder

Functions.Name: SignOfFloat
Functions.Description: Sign (float, returns -1 if A < 0, 0 if A is zero, and +1 if A > 0)

Functions.Name: NormalizeToRange
Functions.Description: Returns Value normalized to the given range.  (e.g. 20 normalized to the range 10->50 would result in 0.25)

Functions.Name: MapRangeUnclamped
Functions.Description: Returns Value mapped from one range into another.  (e.g. 20 normalized from the range 10->50 to 20->40 would result in 25)

Functions.Name: MapRangeClamped
Functions.Description: Returns Value mapped from one range into another where the Value is clamped to the Input Range.  (e.g. 0.5 normalized from the range 0->1 to 0->50 would result in 25)

Functions.Name: MultiplyByPi
Functions.Description:
Multiplies the input value by pi. 
	UFUNCTION(BlueprintPure, meta=(Keywords = " multiply"), Category="Math|Float")

Functions.Name: FInterpEaseInOut
Functions.Description: Interpolate between A and B, applying an ease inout function.  Exp controls the degree of the curve.

Params.Name: InCurrentTime
Params.Description: Current absolute time

Params.Name: InPulsesPerSecond
Params.Description: How many full pulses per second?

Params.Name: InPhase
Params.Description: Optional phase amount, between 0.0 and 1.0 (to synchronize pulses)

Functions.Name: FixedTurn
Functions.Description: Returns a new rotation component value

Params.Name: InCurrent

Params.Name: InDesired

Return.Description: a new rotation component value clamped in the range (-360,360)

Params.Name: ConeHalfAngleInRadians
Params.Description: The half-angle of the cone (from ConeDir to edge), in radians.

Functions.Description: Returns a random vector with length of 1, within the specified cone, with uniform random distribution.

Params.Name: ConeDir
Params.Description: The base "center" direction of the cone.

Params.Name: ConeHalfAngleInDegrees
Params.Description: The half-angle of the cone (from ConeDir to edge), in degrees.

Functions.Name: RandomUnitVectorInEllipticalConeInRadians
Functions.Description:
Returns a random vector with length of 1, within the specified cone, with uniform random distribution.
	 The shape of the cone can be modified according to the yaw and pitch angles.

Params.Name: MaxYawInRadians
Params.Description: The yaw angle of the cone (from ConeDir to horizontal edge), in radians.

Params.Name: MaxPitchInRadians
Params.Description: The pitch angle of the cone (from ConeDir to vertical edge), in radians.

Functions.Name: RandomUnitVectorInEllipticalConeInDegrees
Functions.Description:
Returns a random vector with length of 1, within the specified cone, with uniform random distribution.
	 The shape of the cone can be modified according to the yaw and pitch angles.

Params.Name: MaxYawInDegrees
Params.Description: The yaw angle of the cone (from ConeDir to horizontal edge), in degrees.

Params.Name: MaxPitchInDegrees
Params.Description: The pitch angle of the cone (from ConeDir to vertical edge), in degrees.

Functions.Name: MirrorVectorByNormal
Functions.Description: Mirrors a vector by a normal

Functions.Name: ProjectVectorOnToVector
Functions.Description:
Projects one vector (V) onto another (Target) and returns the projected vector.
	 If Target is nearly zero in length, returns the zero vector.

Params.Name: V
Params.Description: Vector to project.

Params.Name: Target
Params.Description: Vector on which we are projecting.

Functions.Name: GetReflectionVector
Functions.Description:
Given a direction vector and a surface normal, returns the vector reflected across the surface normal.
	  Produces a result like shining a laser at a mirror!

Params.Name: Direction
Params.Description: Direction vector the ray is coming from.

Params.Name: SurfaceNormal
Params.Description: A normal of the surface the ray should be reflected on.

Functions.Name: FindNearestPointsOnLineSegments
Functions.Description: Find closest points between 2 segments.

Params.Name: Segment1Start
Params.Description: Start of the 1st segment.

Params.Name: Segment1End
Params.Description: End of the 1st segment.

Params.Name: Segment2Start
Params.Description: Start of the 2nd segment.

Params.Name: Segment2End
Params.Description: End of the 2nd segment.

Params.Name: Segment1Point
Params.Description: Closest point on segment 1 to segment 2.

Params.Name: Segment2Point
Params.Description: Closest point on segment 2 to segment 1.

Functions.Name: FindClosestPointOnSegment
Functions.Description: Find the closest point on a segment to a given point.

Params.Name: Point
Params.Description: Point for which we find the closest point on the segment.

Params.Name: SegmentStart
Params.Description: Start of the segment.

Params.Name: SegmentEnd
Params.Description: End of the segment.

Return.Description: The closest point on the segment to the given point.

Functions.Name: FindClosestPointOnLine
Functions.Description: Find the closest point on an infinite line to a given point.

Params.Name: Point
Params.Description: Point for which we find the closest point on the line.

Params.Name: LineOrigin
Params.Description: Point of reference on the line.

Params.Name: LineDirection
Params.Description: Direction of the line. Not required to be normalized.

Return.Description: The closest point on the line to the given point.

Functions.Name: GetPointDistanceToSegment
Functions.Description: Find the distance from a point to the closest point on a segment.

Params.Name: Point
Params.Description: Point for which we find the distance to the closest point on the segment.

Params.Name: SegmentStart
Params.Description: Start of the segment.

Params.Name: SegmentEnd
Params.Description: End of the segment.

Return.Description: The distance from the given point to the closest point on the segment.

Functions.Name: GetPointDistanceToLine
Functions.Description: Find the distance from a point to the closest point on an infinite line.

Params.Name: Point
Params.Description: Point for which we find the distance to the closest point on the line.

Params.Name: LineOrigin
Params.Description: Point of reference on the line.

Functions.Name: LinearColorLerp
Functions.Description: Linearly interpolates between A and B based on Alpha (100% of A when Alpha=0 and 100% of B when Alpha=1)

Functions.Name: LinearColorLerpUsingHSV
Functions.Description: Linearly interpolates between two colors by the specified Alpha amount (100% of A when Alpha=0 and 100% of B when Alpha=1).  The interpolation is performed in HSV color space taking the shortest path to the new color's hue.  This can give better results than a normal lerp, but is much more expensive.  The incoming colors are in RGB space, and the output color will be RGB.  The alpha value will also be interpolated.

Params.Name: A
Params.Description: The color and alpha to interpolate from as linear RGBA

Params.Name: B
Params.Description: The color and alpha to interpolate to as linear RGBA

Params.Name: Alpha
Params.Description: Scalar interpolation amount (usually between 0.0 and 1.0 inclusive)

Return.Description: The interpolated color in linear RGB space along with the interpolated alpha value

Functions.Name: MakeRotFromX
Functions.Description: Builds a rotator given only a XAxis. Y and Z are unspecified but will be orthonormal. XAxis need not be normalized.

Functions.Description: Builds a rotation matrix given only a YAxis. X and Z are unspecified but will be orthonormal. YAxis need not be normalized.

Functions.Name: MakeRotFromZ
Functions.Description: Builds a rotation matrix given only a ZAxis. X and Y are unspecified but will be orthonormal. ZAxis need not be normalized.

Functions.Name: MakeRotFromXY
Functions.Description: Builds a matrix with given X and Y axes. X will remain fixed, Y may be changed minimally to enforce orthogonality. Z will be computed. Inputs need not be normalized.

Functions.Name: MakeRotFromXZ
Functions.Description: Builds a matrix with given X and Z axes. X will remain fixed, Z may be changed minimally to enforce orthogonality. Y will be computed. Inputs need not be normalized.

Functions.Name: MakeRotFromYX
Functions.Description: Builds a matrix with given Y and X axes. Y will remain fixed, X may be changed minimally to enforce orthogonality. Z will be computed. Inputs need not be normalized.

Functions.Name: MakeRotFromYZ
Functions.Description: Builds a matrix with given Y and Z axes. Y will remain fixed, Z may be changed minimally to enforce orthogonality. X will be computed. Inputs need not be normalized.

Functions.Name: MakeRotFromZX
Functions.Description: Builds a matrix with given Z and X axes. Z will remain fixed, X may be changed minimally to enforce orthogonality. Y will be computed. Inputs need not be normalized.

Functions.Name: MakeRotFromZY
Functions.Description: Builds a matrix with given Z and Y axes. Z will remain fixed, Y may be changed minimally to enforce orthogonality. X will be computed. Inputs need not be normalized.

Functions.Name: TransformLocation
Functions.Description:
Transform a position by the supplied transform.
	 	For example, if T was an object's transform, this would transform a position from local space to world space.

Functions.Name: TransformDirection
Functions.Description:
Transform a direction vector by the supplied transform - will not change its length. 
	 	For example, if T was an object's transform, this would transform a direction from local space to world space.

Functions.Name: TransformRotation
Functions.Description:
Transform a rotator by the supplied transform. 
	 	For example, if T was an object's transform, this would transform a rotation from local space to world space.

Functions.Name: InverseTransformLocation
Functions.Description:
Transform a position by the inverse of the supplied transform.
	 	For example, if T was an object's transform, this would transform a position from world space to local space.

Functions.Name: InverseTransformDirection
Functions.Description:
Transform a direction vector by the inverse of the supplied transform - will not change its length.
	 	For example, if T was an object's transform, this would transform a direction from world space to local space.

Functions.Name: InverseTransformRotation
Functions.Description:
Transform a rotator by the inverse of the supplied transform. 
	 	For example, if T was an object's transform, this would transform a rotation from world space to local space.

Functions.Name: ComposeTransforms
Functions.Description:
Compose two transforms in order: A  B.
	 
	  Order matters when composing transforms:
	  A  B will yield a transform that logically first applies A then B to any subsequent transformation.
	 
	  Example: LocalToWorld = ComposeTransforms(DeltaRotation, LocalToWorld) will change rotation in local space by DeltaRotation.
	  Example: LocalToWorld = ComposeTransforms(LocalToWorld, DeltaRotation) will change rotation in world space by DeltaRotation.

Functions.Name: ConvertTransformToRelative
Functions.Description:
Returns the given transform, converted to be relative to the given ParentTransform.
	 
	  Example: AToB = ConvertTransformToRelative(AToWorld, BToWorld) to compute A relative to B.

Params.Name: Transform
Params.Description: The transform you wish to convert

Params.Name: ParentTransform
Params.Description: The transform the conversion is relative to (in the same space as Transform)

Functions.Name: InvertTransform
Functions.Description:
Returns the inverse of the given transform T.
	  
	  Example: Given a LocalToWorld transform, WorldToLocal will be returned.

Params.Name: T
Params.Description: The transform you wish to invert

Functions.Name: RandomBoolFromStream
Functions.Description: Returns a random bool

Functions.Name: RandomFloatFromStream
Functions.Description: Returns a random float between 0 and 1

Functions.Name: RandomFloatInRangeFromStream
Functions.Description: Generate a random number between Min and Max

Functions.Name: RandomUnitVectorFromStream
Functions.Description: Returns a random vector with length of 1.0

Functions.Name: RandomRotatorFromStream
Functions.Description: Create a random rotation

Functions.Name: ResetRandomStream
Functions.Description: Reset a random stream

Functions.Name: SeedRandomStream
Functions.Description: Create a new random seed for a random stream

Functions.Name: SetRandomStreamSeed
Functions.Description: Set the seed of a random stream to a specific number

Functions.Name: RandomUnitVectorInConeInRadiansFromStream
Functions.Description: Returns a random vector with length of 1, within the specified cone, with uniform random distribution.

Params.Name: ConeDir
Params.Description: The base "center" direction of the cone.

Params.Name: ConeHalfAngleInRadians
Params.Description: The half-angle of the cone (from ConeDir to edge), in radians.

Params.Name: Stream
Params.Description: The random stream from which to obtain the vector.

Functions.Name: RandomUnitVectorInConeInDegreesFromStream
Functions.Description: Returns a random vector with length of 1, within the specified cone, with uniform random distribution.

Params.Name: ConeDir
Params.Description: The base "center" direction of the cone.

Params.Name: ConeHalfAngleInDegrees
Params.Description: The half-angle of the cone (from ConeDir to edge), in degrees.

Params.Name: Stream
Params.Description: The random stream from which to obtain the vector.

Functions.Name: RandomUnitVectorInEllipticalConeInRadiansFromStream
Functions.Description:
Returns a random vector with length of 1, within the specified cone, with uniform random distribution.
	 The shape of the cone can be modified according to the yaw and pitch angles.

Params.Name: MaxYawInRadians
Params.Description: The yaw angle of the cone (from ConeDir to horizontal edge), in radians.

Params.Name: MaxPitchInRadians
Params.Description: The pitch angle of the cone (from ConeDir to vertical edge), in radians.

Params.Name: Stream
Params.Description: The random stream from which to obtain the vector.

Functions.Name: RandomUnitVectorInEllipticalConeInDegreesFromStream
Functions.Description:
Returns a random vector with length of 1, within the specified cone, with uniform random distribution.
	 The shape of the cone can be modified according to the yaw and pitch angles.

Params.Name: MaxYawInDegrees
Params.Description: The yaw angle of the cone (from ConeDir to horizontal edge), in degrees.

Params.Name: MaxPitchInDegrees
Params.Description: The pitch angle of the cone (from ConeDir to vertical edge), in degrees.

Params.Name: Stream
Params.Description: The random stream from which to obtain the vector.

Functions.Name: MinimumAreaRectangle
Functions.Description: Finds the minimum area rectangle that encloses all of the points in InVerts

Params.Name: InVerts
Params.Description: - Points to enclose in the rectangle

Functions.Name: PointsAreCoplanar
Functions.Description: Determines whether a given set of points are coplanar, with a tolerance. Any three points or less are always coplanar.

Params.Name: Points
Params.Description: - The set of points to determine coplanarity for.

Params.Name: Tolerance
Params.Description: - Larger numbers means more variance is allowed.

Return.Description: Whether the points are relatively coplanar, based on the tolerance

Functions.Name: IsPointInBox
Functions.Description: Determines whether the given point is in a box. Includes points on the box.

Params.Name: Point
Params.Description: Point to test

Params.Name: BoxOrigin
Params.Description: Origin of the box

Params.Name: BoxExtent
Params.Description: Extents of the box (distance in each axis from origin)

Return.Description: Whether the point is in the box.

Functions.Name: IsPointInBoxWithTransform
Functions.Description: Determines whether a given point is in a box with a given transform. Includes points on the box.

Params.Name: Point
Params.Description: Point to test

Params.Name: BoxWorldTransform
Params.Description: Component-to-World transform of the box.

Params.Name: BoxExtent
Params.Description: Extents of the box (distance in each axis from origin), in component space.

Return.Description: Whether the point is in the box.

Functions.Name: LinePlaneIntersection
Functions.Description: Computes the intersection point between a line and a plane.

Params.Name: T
Params.Description: - The t of the intersection between the line and the plane

Params.Name: Intersection
Params.Description: - The point of intersection between the line and the plane

Return.Description: True if the intersection test was successful.

Functions.Name: LinePlaneIntersection_OriginNormal
Functions.Description: Computes the intersection point between a line and a plane.

Params.Name: T
Params.Description: - The t of the intersection between the line and the plane

Params.Name: Intersection
Params.Description: - The point of intersection between the line and the plane

Return.Description: True if the intersection test was successful.
